decks.notes: This deck focuses on controlling the board by using removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay, along with hand disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Duress. The creatures in the deck provide additional control elements, with abilities that help to disrupt opponents' strategies. The mana base is designed to ensure consistent access to all colors of mana needed for the spells in the deck. The goal is to outvalue the opponent by controlling the board state and eventually overwhelming them with powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively play creatures and spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent. By utilizing cards like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, along with burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage, the deck aims to pressure the opponent early and maintain that pressure throughout the game. The deck also features card draw and spell recursion to ensure that it can continue its aggressive playstyle. Overall, the focus is on fast-paced, aggressive gameplay to overwhelm the opponent and secure a swift victory.

The main concept of this Aggro Arclight Phoenix deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with cards like Opt, Sleight of Hand, and Treasure Cruise to bring back Arclight Phoenix from the graveyard for additional damage. The deck also includes cards like Lightning Axe and Fiery Impulse for removal and protection. The deck aims to be fast-paced and aggressive, utilizing efficient card draw and burn spells to control the board while bringing back Arclight Phoenix for repeated attacks.

This deck focuses on using Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can quickly overwhelm opponents. The deck includes cards like Fleeting Spirit and Guardian of New Benalia to support the combo pieces, while also including various removal spells like Fatal Push and Vanishing Verse to control the board. The deck also includes cards like Thoughtseize and Cling to Dust for disruption and card advantage. Overall, the strategy of this deck is to assemble the combo pieces quickly and efficiently to secure victory.

This deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo to generate a powerful engine for victory. It includes cards like Parhelion II, Greasefang Okiba Boss, and Mycosynth Gardens to create a strong, synergistic strategy. The deck also includes cards for control and removal, such as Fatal Push and Thoughtseize, to disrupt the opponent's plans and protect the combo pieces. By combining these elements effectively, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure the win through a combination of synergy and disruption.

This Orzhov Control deck is focused on controlling the game through a combination of removal spells, hand disruption, and board wipes. It utilizes a variety of cards to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building its own resources. The inclusion of the companion Yorion, Sky Nomad allows for extra value by flickering cards for additional benefits. The deck aims to outlast the opponent by maintaining board control and grinding out incremental advantages until it can close out the game with a powerful finisher.

The main concept of this deck is to maintain a fast-paced aggressive strategy by utilizing cheap and efficient creatures like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Emberheart Challenger, supported by burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to clear the way for attacks. The deck also includes cards like Academic Dispute and Experimental Synthesizer to control the board while maintaining pressure. The mana base is built with lands like Steam Vents and Spirebluff Canal to ensure consistent access to both red and blue mana sources for the spells in the deck.

This UR Aggro deck focuses on quickly deploying threats and disrupting the opponent by utilizing spells and aggressive creatures. The main goal is to maintain pressure on the opponent and control the board with cards like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Emberheart Challenger. The deck also includes burn spells like Fiery Impulse and Reckless Rage to clear the way for attacks. With a mix of creatures and spells, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and secure a swift victory.

The deck focuses on control elements and card advantage with a diverse array of multicolored spells to maximize the value of Niv-Mizzet Reborn's ability. It includes a mix of removal, card draw, and ramp spells to disrupt the opponent's game plan while developing your own board presence. The companion Yorion, Sky Nomad provides additional value through its flicker effect. The deck aims to control the game and outvalue the opponent with its versatile and powerful card choices, using Niv-Mizzet Reborn as a key engine for card advantage and threat diversity.

The deck focuses on control elements to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining board presence with creatures like Sentinel of the Nameless City and Ouroboroid. It combines removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay with hand disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Duress to control the game. The inclusion of cards like Unholy Annex / Ritual Chamber and Culling Ritual allows for additional resource management and advantage. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent through a combination of disruption, removal, and resilient threats.

This UW Control deck focuses on gaining and maintaining control of the game through a combination of counterspells, board wipes, and card draw. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while slowly building up its own resources to eventually dominate the late game. With a mix of removal spells, planeswalkers, and utility lands, the deck is well-equipped to handle a variety of threats and strategies. By carefully managing resources and planning ahead, this deck can outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through careful decision-making and strategic plays.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing a mix of counterspells, removal, and card draw. It also includes cards that provide additional value, such as Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive. The companion, Kaheera, the Orphanguard, offers an additional creature to the deck. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win condition through cards like Elspeth, Storm Slayer and Teferi, Hero of Dominaria. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast the opponent in a longer game.

This deck focuses on utilizing the synergy between Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful combo that can quickly overwhelm the opponent. By combining cards like Guardian of New Benalia, Monument to Endurance, and Sheltered by Ghosts, the deck aims to protect its key pieces while setting up for a game-winning turn. Thoughtseize and Fatal Push are included to disrupt the opponent's strategy and maintain control of the game. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to assemble the combo pieces efficiently while controlling the board to secure victory.

The main focus of this UW Control deck is to control the game through counterspells, removal, and card advantage. It aims to disrupt the opponent's plans while setting up its own win conditions, such as resolving powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Elspeth, Storm Slayer. The deck also utilizes card draw and search effects to find key pieces and maintain hand advantage. By controlling the board and out-valuing the opponent, this deck aims to grind out incremental advantages and secure the win in a longer game.

The deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving Greasefang and Parhelion II to overwhelm the opponent. It includes cards like Thoughtseize for disruption, Formidable Speaker for support, and various lands to ensure mana consistency. The main strategy is to control the board with removal spells and creatures while setting up the combo pieces to create an unstoppable force. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Thundering Broodwagon and Cache Grab for additional value and versatility in different situations.

The deck focuses on utilizing the combo between Greasefang and Parhelion II to create a powerful synergy that overwhelms the opponent. It includes cards like Fleeting Spirit and Monument to Endurance to support the combo's strategy, while Thoughtseize and Fatal Push provide disruption and removal to control the game. The mana base is optimized for consistency and efficiency, with key lands like Godless Shrine and Concealed Courtyard. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and outvalue the opponent with resilient threats and powerful interactions.
